summary: Iteration 4 — Calendly webhook + InmoBot combo bundling. Backend: 26/26 pytest cases pass (10 new webhook tests + 16 regression). Webhook accepts unsigned when no key, signs/validates HMAC with key, returns 400 for missing header / wrong v1 / stale timestamp, dedups on (invitee_uuid+event_uuid), and fires a calendly_booking event visible in /api/events/stats. Frontend: Combo section live with title, chat preview and both CTAs (trial -> InmoBot signup w/ utm_source=automatikmedia, learn -> InmoBot home w/ utm); 'Powered by InmoBot' tag visible inside WhatsApp Bots service card (CSS uppercase, DOM text intact); Variant B popup now shows 'InmoBot 7 días gratis' and opens INMOBOT_SIGNUP_URL with utm params, while Variant A still opens Calendly; mobile navbar exposes nav-link-combo; no horizontal overflow at 390px; no console/page errors.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py:262 — except Exception as e is broad; signature path swallows both ValueError and crypto errors as 400. OK for production but a more granular except (ValueError) would let unexpected bugs surface as 500.

server.py:288 — idempotency is only triggered when BOTH invitee_uuid AND event_uuid are present in the payload; a malformed webhook with only one of them would be re-inserted on retry. Calendly always sends both today, but a unique compound index on (invitee_uuid, event_uuid) in MongoDB would harden this.

server.py:293 — calendly_booking event is inserted with name='calendly_booking' but variant=booking.get('event_type') (e.g. 'invitee.created'). This means /api/events/stats will bucket bookings under variants 'invitee.created'/'invitee.canceled', mixing with the A/B variant namespace. Acceptable but worth documenting / using a metadata field instead.
server.py global on_event('shutdown') is deprecated — switch to FastAPI lifespan; not blocking, just a warning.
ExitIntentPopup.jsx still posts /api/events fire-and-forget — same observation as iter-3. Some ad-blockers may block it; covered by stats-counter delta in tests.
test_credentials: N/A — webhook endpoint is public (optional HMAC verification gated by CALENDLY_WEBHOOK_SIGNING_KEY env var)
seed_data_creation: Multiple TEST_-prefixed calendly_bookings inserted via POST /api/webhooks/calendly during webhook tests; a calendly_booking event with variant=invitee.created added per booking; TEST_ leads + A/B/ZTEST events from regression suites.
context_for_next_testing_agent: CALENDLY_WEBHOOK_SIGNING_KEY is NOT set in /app/backend/.env on the running supervisor — webhook accepts unsigned (with WARNING log). Signature-required tests use FastAPI TestClient in-process with os.environ patched + importlib.reload of server module; this does not affect the live backend. Webhook idempotency key = (invitee_uuid, event_uuid). Successful booking also writes an event with name='calendly_booking' and variant=event_type (so it appears under the 'invitee.created' bucket in /api/events/stats, NOT under the A/B variant buckets). Frontend Variant B no longer opens wa.me — it opens INMOBOT_SIGNUP_URL (https://inmobot-ia.com/signup?utm_source=automatikmedia&utm_medium=landing&utm_campaign=combo). Patch window.open to capture calls instead of waiting for a new tab. 'Powered by InmoBot' is CSS-uppercased ('POWERED BY INMOBOT' in innerText) — use case-insensitive matching.
rca of the issue: No issues found. All acceptance criteria from the iter-4 spec verified end-to-end.
